title: Committed review uses authoritative PR scope instead of branch-only commits
description: A sanitized committed-review scenario where explicit PR context exists, but the local branch also contains unrelated branch-only commits. The correct review must treat the PR changed-file set as authoritative and must not report findings from branch-only files.

scope.notes: The authoritative PR scope contains one provider Go file. The local branch also contains an unrelated docs-only commit that is intentionally outside PR scope and must not leak into committed-review findings.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should treat explicit PR metadata as the authoritative review scope instead of reviewing the whole branch diff.
The review should describe the committed review as PR-scoped and keep the files-changed and analysis sections consistent with that scope.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not raise findings against branch-only files such as `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md` when that file is outside the authoritative pull request scope.
The review must not describe the committed review scope as a generic `origin/main...HEAD` branch diff when explicit PR context is available.

notes: Pair this case with the sample review Markdown output and scored result to benchmark PR-authoritative committed-review scope selection.
